briefdescription.para: XML_PARSER_CHUNK_SIZE.

detaileddescription.para: When calling GROW that's the minimal amount of data the parser expected to have received. It is not a hard limit but an optimization when reading strings like Names It is not strictly needed as long as inputs available characters are followed by 0, which should be provided by the I/O level

memberdef.name: CMP4
memberdef.initializer: ( ((unsigned char *) s)[ 0 ] == c1 && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 1 ] == c2 && \ ((unsigned char *) s)[ 2 ] == c3 && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 3 ] == c4 )

memberdef.name: CMP5
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP4( s, c1, c2, c3, c4 ) && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 4 ] == c5 )

memberdef.name: CMP6
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP5( s, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5 ) && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 5 ] == c6 )

memberdef.name: CMP7
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP6( s, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6 ) && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 6 ] == c7 )

memberdef.name: CMP8
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP7( s, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7 ) && ((unsigned char *) s)[ 7 ] == c8 )

memberdef.name: CMP9
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP8( s, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 ) && \ ((unsigned char *) s)[ 8 ] == c9 )

memberdef.name: CMP10
memberdef.initializer: ( CMP9( s, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, c9 ) && \ ((unsigned char *) s)[ 9 ] == c10 )

memberdef.name: SKIPL
memberdef.initializer: do { \ int skipl; \ for(skipl=0; skipl<val; skipl++) { \ if (*(ctxt->input->cur) == '\n') { \ ctxt->input->line++; ctxt->input->col = 1; \ } else ctxt->input->col++; \ ctxt->input->cur++; \ } \ if (*ctxt->input->cur == 0) \ xmlParserGrow(ctxt); \ } while (0)

memberdef.name: NEXTL
COPY_BUF
memberdef.initializer: if (v < 0x80) b[i++] = v; \ else i += xmlCopyCharMultiByte(&b[i],v)

briefdescription.para: Constant string describing the version of the library used at run-time.
Parse the start of an XML element.

detaileddescription.para: Returns -1 in case of error, 0 if an opening tag was parsed, 1 if an empty element was parsed.
Always consumes '<'.

briefdescription.para: Parse the end of an XML element.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '</'.

briefdescription.para: Parse an entity reference.

detaileddescription.para:
Always consumes '&'. [68] EntityRef ::= '&' Name ';'
ctxt
an XML parser context
the name, or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Parse PEReference declarations.

detaileddescription.para: [69] PEReference ::= '%' Name ';' [ WFC: No Recursion ] A parsed entity must not contain a recursive reference to itself, either directly or indirectly.

[ WFC: Entity Declared ] In a document without any DTD, a document with only an internal DTD subset which contains no parameter entity references, or a document with "standalone='yes'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ VC: Entity Declared ] In a document with an external subset or external parameter entities with "standalone='no'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ WFC: In DTD ] Parameter-entity references may only appear in the DTD. NOTE: misleading but this is handled.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an index in the string

simplesect.para: the string of the entity content. str is updated to the current value of the index

briefdescription.para: Load the content of an entity.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Parse a parameter entity reference.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes ''.

The entity content is handled directly by pushing it's content as a new input stream. [69] PEReference ::= '%' Name ';' [ WFC: No Recursion ] A parsed entity must not contain a recursive reference to itself, either directly or indirectly.

[ WFC: Entity Declared ] In a document without any DTD, a document with only an internal DTD subset which contains no parameter entity references, or a document with "standalone='yes'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ VC: Entity Declared ] In a document with an external subset or external parameter entities with "standalone='no'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ WFC: In DTD ] Parameter-entity references may only appear in the DTD. NOTE: misleading but this is handled.

parameterdescription.para: whether the PERef starts a markup declaration

briefdescription.para: Handle a redefinition of attribute error.
Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *str1, const xmlChar *str2)
memberdef.name: xmlWarningMsg

briefdescription.para: Handle a warning.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *str1, const xmlChar *str2)
memberdef.name: xmlValidityError

briefdescription.para: Handle a validity error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, int val)
memberdef.name: xmlFatalErrMsgInt

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *str1, int val, const xmlChar *str2)
memberdef.name: xmlFatalErrMsgStrIntStr

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *val)
memberdef.name: xmlFatalErrMsgStr

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *val)
memberdef.name: xmlErrMsgStr

briefdescription.para: Handle a non fatal parser error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *info1, const xmlChar *info2, const xmlChar *info3)
memberdef.name: xmlNsErr

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *info1, const xmlChar *info2, const xmlChar *info3)
memberdef.name: xmlNsWarn

briefdescription.para: Handle a namespace warning error.
Check for non-linear entity expansion behaviour.

detaileddescription.para: In some cases like xmlExpandEntityInAttValue, this function is called for each, possibly nested entity and its unexpanded content length.

In other cases like xmlParseReference, it's only called for each top-level entity with its unexpanded content length plus the sum of the unexpanded content lengths (plus fixed cost) of all nested entities.

Summing the unexpanded lengths also adds the length of the reference. This is by design. Taking the length of the entity name into account discourages attacks that try to waste CPU time with abusively long entity names. See test/recurse/lol6.xml for example. Each call also adds some fixed cost XML_ENT_FIXED_COST to discourage attacks with short entities.

simplesect.para: 1 on error, 0 on success.

briefdescription.para: Examines if the library has been compiled with a given feature.

simplesect.para: zero (0) if the feature does not exist or an unknown feature is requested, non-zero otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Final initialization of the parser context before starting to parse.

detaileddescription.para: This accounts for users modifying struct members of parser context directly.

briefdescription.para: Normalize the space in non CDATA attribute values: If the attribute type is not CDATA, then the XML processor MUST further process the normalized attribute value by discarding any leading and trailing space (#x20) characters, and by replacing sequences of space (#x20) characters by a single space (#x20) character.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the size of dst need to be at least src, and if one doesn't need to preserve dst (and it doesn't come from a dictionary or read-only) then passing src as dst is just fine.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the normalized value (dst) or NULL if no conversion is needed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *fullname, const xmlChar *fullattr, const xmlChar *value)
memberdef.name: xmlAddDefAttrs

briefdescription.para: Add a defaulted attribute for an element.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *fullname, const xmlChar *fullattr, int type)
memberdef.name: xmlAddSpecialAttr

briefdescription.para: Register this attribute type.

memberdef.argsstring: (void *payload, void *data, const xmlChar *fullname, const xmlChar *fullattr, const xmlChar *unused)
memberdef.name: xmlCleanSpecialAttrCallback

briefdescription.para: Removes CDATA attributes from the special attribute table.
Trim the list of attributes defined to remove all those of type CDATA as they are not special.

detaileddescription.para: This call should be done when finishing to parse the DTD and before starting to parse the document root.

briefdescription.para: Checks that the value conforms to the LanguageID production:

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: this is somewhat deprecated, those productions were removed from the XML Second edition. [33] LanguageID ::= Langcode ('-' Subcode)* [34] Langcode ::= ISO639Code | IanaCode | UserCode [35] ISO639Code ::= ([a-z] | [A-Z]) ([a-z] | [A-Z]) [36] IanaCode ::= ('i' | 'I') '-' ([a-z] | [A-Z])+ [37] UserCode ::= ('x' | 'X') '-' ([a-z] | [A-Z])+ [38] Subcode ::= ([a-z] | [A-Z])+ The current REC reference the successors of RFC 1766, currently 5646

http://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5646.txt langtag = language ["-" script] ["-" region] *("-" variant) *("-" extension) ["-" privateuse] language = 2*3ALPHA ; shortest ISO 639 code ["-" extlang] ; sometimes followed by ; extended language subtags / 4ALPHA ; or reserved for future use / 5*8ALPHA ; or registered language subtag
extlang = 3ALPHA ; selected ISO 639 codes *2("-" 3ALPHA) ; permanently reserved
script = 4ALPHA ; ISO 15924 code
region = 2ALPHA ; ISO 3166-1 code / 3DIGIT ; UN M.49 code
variant = 5*8alphanum ; registered variants / (DIGIT 3alphanum)
extension = singleton 1*("-" (2*8alphanum))
; Single alphanumerics ; "x" reserved for private use singleton = DIGIT ; 0 - 9 / %x41-57 ; A - W / %x59-5A ; Y - Z / %x61-77 ; a - w / %x79-7A ; y - z it sounds right to still allow Irregular i-xxx IANA and user codes too The parser below doesn't try to cope with extension or privateuse that could be added but that's not interoperable anyway

simplesect.para: 1 if correct 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse ENTITY references declarations, but this version parses it from a string value.

detaileddescription.para: [68] EntityRef ::= '&' Name ';' [ WFC: Entity Declared ] In a document without any DTD, a document with only an internal DTD subset which contains no parameter entity references, or a document with "standalone='yes'", the Name given in the entity reference must match that in an entity declaration, except that well-formed documents need not declare any of the following entities: amp, lt, gt, apos, quot. The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it. Similarly, the declaration of a general entity must precede any reference to it which appears in a default value in an attribute-list declaration. Note that if entities are declared in the external subset or in external parameter entities, a non-validating processor is not obligated to read and process their declarations; for such documents, the rule that an entity must be declared is a well-formedness constraint only if standalone='yes'.
[ WFC: Parsed Entity ] An entity reference must not contain the name of an unparsed entity

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an index in the string

simplesect.para: the xmlEntity if found, or NULL otherwise. The str pointer is updated to the current location in the string.

briefdescription.para: Create a new namespace database.

simplesect.para: the new obejct.

briefdescription.para: Free a namespace database.
Reset a namespace database.
Signal that a new element has started.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if the element counter overflowed.

briefdescription.para: Lookup namespace with given prefix.

detaileddescription.para: If bucketPtr is non-NULL, it will be set to the matching bucket, or the first empty bucket if no match was found.

simplesect.para: the namespace index on success, INT_MAX if no namespace was found.

briefdescription.para: Lookup namespace URI with given prefix.

simplesect.para: the namespace URI on success, NULL if no namespace was found.

briefdescription.para: Lookup extra data for the given prefix.

detaileddescription.para: This returns data stored with xmlParserNsUdpateSax.

simplesect.para: the data on success, NULL if no namespace was found.

briefdescription.para: Sets or updates extra data for the given prefix.

detaileddescription.para: This value will be returned by xmlParserNsLookupSax as long as the namespace with the given prefix is in scope.

simplesect.para: the data on success, NULL if no namespace was found.

briefdescription.para: Grows the namespace tables.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlHashedString *prefix, const xmlHashedString *uri, void *saxData, int defAttr)
memberdef.name: xmlParserNsPush

briefdescription.para: Push a new namespace on the table.

parameterdescription.para: whether the namespace comes from a default attribute

simplesect.para: 1 if the namespace was pushed, 0 if the namespace was ignored, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Pops the top nr namespaces and restores the hash table.

simplesect.para: the number of namespaces popped.

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new parser input on top of the input stack.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top parser input from the input stack.

simplesect.para: the input just removed

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new element node on top of the node stack.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element node from the node stack.

simplesect.para: the node just removed

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *value, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI, int line, int nsNr)
memberdef.name: nameNsPush

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new element name/prefix/URL on top of the name stack.

parameterdescription.para: the current line number for error messages
the number of namespaces pushed on the namespace table

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element/prefix/URI name from the name stack.

simplesect.para: the name just removed

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element name from the name stack.

simplesect.para: the name just removed

briefdescription.para: Skip whitespace in the input stream.

simplesect.para: the number of space chars skipped

briefdescription.para: Skip whitespace in the input stream, also handling parameter entities.

simplesect.para: the number of space chars skipped
the current xmlChar in the parser context

briefdescription.para: Push an input stream onto the stack.

parameterdescription.para: an XML parser input fragment (entity, XML fragment ...).

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error or the index in the input stack

briefdescription.para: Parse a numeric character reference.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '&'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [66] CharRef ::= '&#' [0-9]+ ';' | '&#x' [0-9a-fA-F]+ ';' [ WFC: Legal Character ] Characters referred to using character references must match the production for Char.

simplesect.para: the value parsed (as an int), 0 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse Reference declarations, variant parsing from a string rather than an an input flow.

detaileddescription.para: [66] CharRef ::= '&#' [0-9]+ ';' | '&#x' [0-9a-fA-F]+ ';' [ WFC: Legal Character ] Characters referred to using character references must match the production for Char.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an index in the string

simplesect.para: the value parsed (as an int), 0 in case of error, str will be updated to the current value of the index

detaileddescription.para: [ WFC: No Recursion ] A parsed entity must not contain a recursive reference to itself, either directly or indirectly.

[ WFC: Entity Declared ] In a document without any DTD, a document with only an internal DTD subset which contains no parameter entity references, or a document with "standalone='yes'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ VC: Entity Declared ] In a document with an external subset or external parameter entities with "standalone='no'", ... ... The declaration of a parameter entity must precede any reference to it...
[ WFC: In DTD ] Parameter-entity references may only appear in the DTD. NOTE: misleading but this is handled.

A PEReference may have been detected in the current input stream the handling is done accordingly to http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-xml#entproc i.e.
Included in literal in entity values
Included as Parameter Entity reference within DTDs
ctxt
the parser context

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar *str, int len, int what, xmlChar end, xmlChar end2, xmlChar end3)
memberdef.name: xmlStringLenDecodeEntities

parameterdescription.para: an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none
an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none
an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none

simplesect.para: A newly allocated string with the substitution done. The caller must deallocate it !

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar *str, int what, xmlChar end, xmlChar end2, xmlChar end3)
memberdef.name: xmlStringDecodeEntities

parameterdescription.para: an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none
an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none
an end marker xmlChar, 0 if none

simplesect.para: A newly allocated string with the substitution done. The caller must deallocate it !

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *str, int len, int blank_chars)
memberdef.name: areBlanks

briefdescription.para: Is this a sequence of blank chars that one can ignore ?

parameterdescription.para: we know the chars are blanks

simplesect.para: 1 if ignorable 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse an UTF8 encoded XML qualified name string.

simplesect.para: the local part, and prefix is updated to get the Prefix if any.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML name.

para.verbatim:
[4] NameChar ::= Letter | Digit | '.' | '-' | '_' | ':' | CombiningChar | Extender
[5] Name ::= (Letter | '_' | ':') (NameChar)*
[6] Names ::= Name (#x20 Name)*

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML name.

para.verbatim:
[4NS] NCNameChar ::= Letter | Digit | '.' | '-' | '_' | CombiningChar | Extender
[5NS] NCName ::= (Letter | '_') (NCNameChar)*

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML name and compares for match (specialized for endtag parsing)

simplesect.para: NULL for an illegal name, (xmlChar*) 1 for success and the name for mismatch

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML name.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the string pointer (IN/OUT)

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL. The str pointer is updated to the current location in the string.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Nmtoken.

simplesect.para: the Nmtoken parsed or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlSBuf *buf, const xmlChar *str, int length, int depth)
memberdef.name: xmlExpandPEsInEntityValue

briefdescription.para: Validate an entity value and expand parameter entities.
Parse a value for ENTITY declarations.

parameterdescription.para: if non-NULL store a copy of the original entity value

simplesect.para: the EntityValue parsed with reference substituted or NULL

briefdescription.para: Check an entity reference in an attribute value for validity without expanding it.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlSBuf *buf, const xmlChar *str, xmlEntityPtr pent, int normalize, int *inSpace, int depth, int check)
memberdef.name: xmlExpandEntityInAttValue

briefdescription.para: Expand general entity references in an entity or attribute value.

detaileddescription.para: Perform attribute value normalization.

parameterdescription.para: entity for entity value, NULL for attribute values

simplesect.para: whether there was a normalization change

briefdescription.para: Expand general entity references in an entity or attribute value.

detaileddescription.para: Perform attribute value normalization.

simplesect.para: the expanded attribtue value.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, int *attlen, int *outFlags, int special, int isNamespace)
memberdef.name: xmlParseAttValueInternal

briefdescription.para: Parse a value for an attribute.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: if no normalization is needed, the routine will return pointers directly from the data buffer.
3.3.3 Attribute-Value Normalization:
Before the value of an attribute is passed to the application or checked for validity, the XML processor must normalize it as follows:

listitem.para: a character reference is processed by appending the referenced character to the attribute value
an entity reference is processed by recursively processing the replacement text of the entity

a whitespace character (#x20, #xD, #xA, #x9) is processed by appending #x20 to the normalized value, except that only a single #x20 is appended for a "#xD#xA" sequence that is part of an external parsed entity or the literal entity value of an internal parsed entity
other characters are processed by appending them to the normalized value

detaileddescription.para: If the declared value is not CDATA, then the XML processor must further process the normalized attribute value by discarding any leading and trailing space (#x20) characters, and by replacing sequences of space (#x20) characters by a single space (#x20) character. All attributes for which no declaration has been read should be treated by a non-validating parser as if declared CDATA.

parameterdescription.para: whether this is a namespace declaration

simplesect.para: the AttValue parsed or NULL. The value has to be freed by the caller if it was copied, this can be detected by val[*len] == 0.

briefdescription.para: Parse a value for an attribute Note: the parser won't do substitution of entities here, this will be handled later in xmlStringGetNodeList.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [10] AttValue ::= '"' ([^<&"] | Reference)* '"' | "'" ([^<&'] | Reference)* "'" 3.3.3 Attribute-Value Normalization:
Before the value of an attribute is passed to the application or checked for validity, the XML processor must normalize it as follows:

listitem.para: a character reference is processed by appending the referenced character to the attribute value
an entity reference is processed by recursively processing the replacement text of the entity

a whitespace character (#x20, #xD, #xA, #x9) is processed by appending #x20 to the normalized value, except that only a single #x20 is appended for a "#xD#xA" sequence that is part of an external parsed entity or the literal entity value of an internal parsed entity
other characters are processed by appending them to the normalized value

detaileddescription.para: If the declared value is not CDATA, then the XML processor must further process the normalized attribute value by discarding any leading and trailing space (#x20) characters, and by replacing sequences of space (#x20) characters by a single space (#x20) character. All attributes for which no declaration has been read should be treated by a non-validating parser as if declared CDATA.

simplesect.para: the AttValue parsed or NULL. The value has to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Literal.

simplesect.para: the SystemLiteral parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML public literal.

simplesect.para: the PubidLiteral parsed or NULL.

briefdescription.para: Always makes progress if the first char isn't '<' or '&'.

detaileddescription.para: parse a CharData section.this is the fallback function of xmlParseCharData when the parsing requires handling of non-ASCII characters.

parameterdescription.para: whether the input can end with truncated UTF-8

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *buf, int size, int isBlank)
memberdef.name: xmlCharacters

briefdescription.para: Parse character data.

detaileddescription.para: Always makes progress if the first char isn't '<' or '&'.

The right angle bracket (>) may be represented using the string ">", and must, for compatibility, be escaped using ">" or a character reference when it appears in the string "]]>" in content, when that string is not marking the end of a CDATA section. [14] CharData ::= [^<&]* - ([^<&]* ']]>' [^<&]*)
ctxt
an XML parser context
partial
buffer may contain partial UTF-8 sequences

briefdescription.para: Parse an External ID or a Public ID.

detaileddescription.para:
NOTE: Productions [75] and [83] interact badly since [75] can generate 'PUBLIC S PubidLiteral S SystemLiteral [75] ExternalID ::= 'SYSTEM’ S SystemLiteral | 'PUBLIC' S PubidLiteral S SystemLiteral
[83] PublicID ::= 'PUBLIC' S PubidLiteral
ctxt
an XML parser context
publicId
a xmlChar** receiving PubidLiteral
strict
indicate whether we should restrict parsing to only production [75], see NOTE below
the function returns SystemLiteral and in the second case publicID receives PubidLiteral, is strict is off it is possible to return NULL and have publicID set.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlChar *buf, size_t len, size_t size)
memberdef.name: xmlParseCommentComplex

briefdescription.para: Skip an XML (SGML) comment The spec says that "For compatibility, the string "" (double-hyphen) must not occur within comments.

detaileddescription.para:
" This is the slow routine in case the accelerator for ascii didn't work [15] Comment ::= '<!--' ((Char - '-') | ('-' (Char - '-')))* '-->'
ctxt
an XML parser context
buf
the already parsed part of the buffer
len
number of bytes in the buffer
size
allocated size of the buffer

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML (SGML) comment.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!'.
The spec says that "For compatibility, the string "" (double-hyphen) must not occur within comments. "

[15] Comment ::= ''
ctxt
an XML parser context

briefdescription.para: Parse the name of a PI.

simplesect.para: the PITarget name or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Catalog Processing Instruction.

detaileddescription.para: <?oasis-xml-catalog catalog="http://example.com/catalog.xml"?>

Occurs only if allowed by the user and if happening in the Misc part of the document before any doctype information This will add the given catalog to the parsing context in order to be used if there is a resolution need further down in the document

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Processing Instruction.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [16] PI ::= '<?' PITarget (S (Char* - (Char* '?>' Char*)))? '?>' The processing is transferred to SAX once parsed.

briefdescription.para: Parse a notation declaration.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [82] NotationDecl ::= '<!NOTATION' S Name S (ExternalID | PublicID) S? '>' Hence there is actually 3 choices: 'PUBLIC' S PubidLiteral 'PUBLIC' S PubidLiteral S SystemLiteral 'SYSTEM' S SystemLiteral See the NOTE on xmlParseExternalID.

briefdescription.para: Parse an entity declaration.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [70] EntityDecl ::= GEDecl | PEDecl
[71] GEDecl ::= '<!ENTITY' S Name S EntityDef S? '>'
[72] PEDecl ::= '<!ENTITY' S '%' S Name S PEDef S? '>'
[73] EntityDef ::= EntityValue | (ExternalID NDataDecl?)
[74] PEDef ::= EntityValue | ExternalID
[76] NDataDecl ::= S 'NDATA' S Name [ VC: Notation Declared ] The Name must match the declared name of a notation.

briefdescription.para: Parse an attribute default declaration.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [60] DefaultDecl ::= '#REQUIRED' | '#IMPLIED' | (('#FIXED' S)? AttValue) [ VC: Required Attribute ] if the default declaration is the keyword #REQUIRED, then the attribute must be specified for all elements of the type in the attribute-list declaration.
[ VC: Attribute Default Legal ] The declared default value must meet the lexical constraints of the declared attribute type c.f. xmlValidateAttributeDecl
[ VC: Fixed Attribute Default ] if an attribute has a default value declared with the #FIXED keyword, instances of that attribute must match the default value.
[ WFC: No < in Attribute Values ] handled in xmlParseAttValue

parameterdescription.para: Receive a possible fixed default value for the attribute

simplesect.para: XML_ATTRIBUTE_NONE, XML_ATTRIBUTE_REQUIRED, XML_ATTRIBUTE_IMPLIED or XML_ATTRIBUTE_FIXED.

briefdescription.para: Parse an Notation attribute type.

detaileddescription.para: Note: the leading 'NOTATION' S part has already being parsed... [58] NotationType ::= 'NOTATION' S '(' S? Name (S? '|' S? Name)* S? ')' [ VC: Notation Attributes ] Values of this type must match one of the notation names included in the declaration; all notation names in the declaration must be declared.

simplesect.para: the notation attribute tree built while parsing

briefdescription.para: Parse an Enumeration attribute type.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [59] Enumeration ::= '(' S? Nmtoken (S? '|' S? Nmtoken)* S? ')' [ VC: Enumeration ] Values of this type must match one of the Nmtoken tokens in the declaration

simplesect.para: the enumeration attribute tree built while parsing

briefdescription.para: Parse an Enumerated attribute type.

para.verbatim:
[57] EnumeratedType ::= NotationType | Enumeration
[58] NotationType ::= 'NOTATION' S '(' S? Name (S? '|' S? Name)* S? ')'

parameterdescription.para: the enumeration tree built while parsing

simplesect.para: XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION or XML_ATTRIBUTE_NOTATION

briefdescription.para: Parse the Attribute list def for an element.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [54] AttType ::= StringType | TokenizedType | EnumeratedType
[55] StringType ::= 'CDATA'
[56] TokenizedType ::= 'ID' | 'IDREF' | 'IDREFS' | 'ENTITY' | 'ENTITIES' | 'NMTOKEN' | 'NMTOKENS' Validity constraints for attribute values syntax are checked in xmlValidateAttributeValue

[ VC: ID ] Values of type ID must match the Name production. A name must not appear more than once in an XML document as a value of this type; i.e., ID values must uniquely identify the elements which bear them.
[ VC: One ID per Element Type ] No element type may have more than one ID attribute specified.
[ VC: ID Attribute Default ] An ID attribute must have a declared default of #IMPLIED or #REQUIRED.

[ VC: IDREF ] Values of type IDREF must match the Name production, and values of type IDREFS must match Names; each IDREF Name must match the value of an ID attribute on some element in the XML document; i.e. IDREF values must match the value of some ID attribute.
[ VC: Entity Name ] Values of type ENTITY must match the Name production, values of type ENTITIES must match Names; each Entity Name must match the name of an unparsed entity declared in the DTD.
[ VC: Name Token ] Values of type NMTOKEN must match the Nmtoken production; values of type NMTOKENS must match Nmtokens.

parameterdescription.para: the enumeration tree built while parsing

simplesect.para: the attribute type

briefdescription.para: Parse an attribute list declaration for an element.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!'.

para.verbatim:
[52] AttlistDecl ::= '<!ATTLIST' S Name AttDef* S? '>'
[53] AttDef ::= S Name S AttType S DefaultDecl

briefdescription.para: Handle PEs and check that we don't pop the entity that started a balanced group.

parameterdescription.para: input nr of the entity with opening '('

briefdescription.para: Parse the declaration for a Mixed Element content The leading '(' and spaces have been skipped in xmlParseElementContentDecl.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [51] Mixed ::= '(' S? '#PCDATA' (S? '|' S? Name)* S? ')*' | '(' S? '#PCDATA' S? ')' [ VC: Proper Group/PE Nesting ] applies to [51] too (see [49])
[ VC: No Duplicate Types ] The same name must not appear more than once in a single mixed-content declaration.

parameterdescription.para: the input used for the current entity, needed for boundary checks

simplesect.para: the list of the xmlElementContent describing the element choices

briefdescription.para: Parse the declaration for a Mixed Element content The leading '(' and spaces have been skipped in xmlParseElementContentDecl.

detaileddescription.para:
[47] children ::= (choice | seq) ('?' | '*' | '+')?
[48] cp ::= (Name | choice | seq) ('?' | '*' | '+')?
[49] choice ::= '(' S? cp ( S? '|' S? cp )* S? ')'
[50] seq ::= '(' S? cp ( S? ',' S? cp )* S? ')' [ VC: Proper Group/PE Nesting ] applies to [49] and [50] TODO Parameter-entity replacement text must be properly nested with parenthesized groups. That is to say, if either of the opening or closing parentheses in a choice, seq, or Mixed construct is contained in the replacement text for a parameter entity, both must be contained in the same replacement text. For interoperability, if a parameter-entity reference appears in a choice, seq, or Mixed construct, its replacement text should not be empty, and neither the first nor last non-blank character of the replacement text should be a connector (| or ,).

parameterdescription.para: the input used for the current entity, needed for boundary checks

simplesect.para: the tree of xmlElementContent describing the element hierarchy.

briefdescription.para: Parse the declaration for a Mixed Element content The leading '(' and spaces have been skipped in xmlParseElementContentDecl.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [47] children ::= (choice | seq) ('?' | '*' | '+')?
[48] cp ::= (Name | choice | seq) ('?' | '*' | '+')?
[49] choice ::= '(' S? cp ( S? '|' S? cp )* S? ')'
[50] seq ::= '(' S? cp ( S? ',' S? cp )* S? ')' [ VC: Proper Group/PE Nesting ] applies to [49] and [50] TODO Parameter-entity replacement text must be properly nested with parenthesized groups. That is to say, if either of the opening or closing parentheses in a choice, seq, or Mixed construct is contained in the replacement text for a parameter entity, both must be contained in the same replacement text. For interoperability, if a parameter-entity reference appears in a choice, seq, or Mixed construct, its replacement text should not be empty, and neither the first nor last non-blank character of the replacement text should be a connector (| or ,).

parameterdescription.para: the input used for the current entity, needed for boundary checks

simplesect.para: the tree of xmlElementContent describing the element hierarchy.

briefdescription.para: Parse the declaration for an Element content either Mixed or Children, the cases EMPTY and ANY are handled directly in xmlParseElementDecl.

parameterdescription.para: the name of the element being defined.
the Element Content pointer will be stored here if any

simplesect.para: an xmlElementTypeVal value or -1 on error

briefdescription.para: Parse an element declaration.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [45] elementdecl ::= '<!ELEMENT' S Name S contentspec S? '>' [ VC: Unique Element Type Declaration ] No element type may be declared more than once

simplesect.para: the type of the element, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse a conditional section.

detaileddescription.para:
Always consumes '<!['. [61] conditionalSect ::= includeSect | ignoreSect [62] includeSect ::= '<![' S? 'INCLUDE' S? '[' extSubsetDecl ']]>' [63] ignoreSect ::= '<![' S? 'IGNORE' S? '[' ignoreSectContents* ']]>' [64] ignoreSectContents ::= Ignore ('<![' ignoreSectContents ']]>' Ignore)* [65] Ignore ::= Char* - (Char* ('<![' | ']]>') Char*)
ctxt
an XML parser context

briefdescription.para: Parse markup declarations.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!' or '<?'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [29] markupdecl ::= elementdecl | AttlistDecl | EntityDecl | NotationDecl | PI | Comment [ VC: Proper Declaration/PE Nesting ] Parameter-entity replacement text must be properly nested with markup declarations. That is to say, if either the first character or the last character of a markup declaration (markupdecl above) is contained in the replacement text for a parameter-entity reference, both must be contained in the same replacement text.

[ WFC: PEs in Internal Subset ] In the internal DTD subset, parameter-entity references can occur only where markup declarations can occur, not within markup declarations. (This does not apply to references that occur in external parameter entities or to the external subset.)

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML declaration header for external entities.
Parse Markup declarations from an external subset.

para.verbatim:
[30] extSubset ::= textDecl? extSubsetDecl
[31] extSubsetDecl ::= (markupdecl | conditionalSect | PEReference | S) *

briefdescription.para: Parse and handle entity references in content, depending on the SAX interface, this may end-up in a call to character() if this is a CharRef, a predefined entity, if there is no reference() callback.

detaileddescription.para: or if the parser was asked to switch to that mode.

Always consumes '&'. [67] Reference ::= EntityRef | CharRef
ctxt
an XML parser context

simplesect.para: the xmlEntity if found, or NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse a parameter entity reference.
Parse a DOCTYPE declaration.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [28] doctypedecl ::= '<!DOCTYPE' S Name (S ExternalID)? S? ('[' (markupdecl | PEReference | S)* ']' S?)? '>' [ VC: Root Element Type ] The Name in the document type declaration must match the element type of the root element.

briefdescription.para: Parse the internal subset declaration.
Parse an attribute.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [41] Attribute ::= Name Eq AttValue [ WFC: No External Entity References ] Attribute values cannot contain direct or indirect entity references to external entities.
[ WFC: No < in Attribute Values ] The replacement text of any entity referred to directly or indirectly in an attribute value (other than "<") must not contain a <.

[ VC: Attribute Value Type ] The attribute must have been declared; the value must be of the type declared for it. [25] Eq ::= S? '=' S? With namespace: [NS 11] Attribute ::= QName Eq AttValue Also the case QName == xmlns:??? is handled independently as a namespace definition.

parameterdescription.para: a xmlChar ** used to store the value of the attribute

simplesect.para: the attribute name, and the value in *value.

briefdescription.para: Parse a start tag.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<'.

Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [40] STag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '>' [ WFC: Unique Att Spec ] No attribute name may appear more than once in the same start-tag or empty-element tag. [44] EmptyElemTag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '/>' [ WFC: Unique Att Spec ] No attribute name may appear more than once in the same start-tag or empty-element tag.

With namespace: [NS 8] STag ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '>'
[NS 10] EmptyElement ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '/>'
ctxt
an XML parser context
the element name parsed

briefdescription.para: Parse an end tag.

detaileddescription.para:
Always consumes '</'. [42] ETag ::= '</' Name S? '>' With namespace [NS 9] ETag ::= '</' QName S? '>'
ctxt
an XML parser context
line
line of the start tag

briefdescription.para: Parse an end of tag.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [42] ETag ::= '</' Name S? '>' With namespace [NS 9] ETag ::= '</' QName S? '>'
ctxt
an XML parser context

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Namespace QName.

para.verbatim: [6] QName ::= (Prefix ':')? LocalPart [7] Prefix ::= NCName [8] LocalPart ::= NCName

parameterdescription.para: pointer to store the prefix part

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Namespace QName.

para.verbatim: [6] QName ::= (Prefix ':')? LocalPart [7] Prefix ::= NCName [8] LocalPart ::= NCName

parameterdescription.para: pointer to store the prefix part

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML name and compares for match (specialized for endtag parsing)

simplesect.para: NULL for an illegal name, (xmlChar*) 1 for success and the name for mismatch

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *pref, const xmlChar *elem, xmlHashedString *hprefix, xmlChar **value, int *len, int *alloc)
memberdef.name: xmlParseAttribute2

briefdescription.para: Parse an attribute in the new SAX2 framework.

parameterdescription.para: resulting indicator if the attribute was allocated

simplesect.para: the attribute name, and the value in *value, .

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, unsigned size, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *uri, unsigned hashValue, int aindex)
memberdef.name: xmlAttrHashInsert

briefdescription.para: Inserts a new attribute into the hash table.

parameterdescription.para: combined hash value of name and uri
attribute index (this is a multiple of 5)

simplesect.para: INT_MAX if no existing attribute was found, the attribute index if an attribute was found, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, unsigned size, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *prefix, unsigned hashValue, int aindex)
memberdef.name: xmlAttrHashInsertQName

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar **pref, const xmlChar **URI, int *nbNsPtr)
memberdef.name: xmlParseStartTag2

briefdescription.para: Parse a start tag.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<'.

This routine is called when running SAX2 parsing [40] STag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '>' [ WFC: Unique Att Spec ] No attribute name may appear more than once in the same start-tag or empty-element tag. [44] EmptyElemTag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '/>' [ WFC: Unique Att Spec ] No attribute name may appear more than once in the same start-tag or empty-element tag.

With namespace: [NS 8] STag ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '>'
[NS 10] EmptyElement ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '/>'
ctxt
an XML parser context
pref
resulting namespace prefix
URI
resulting namespace URI
nbNsPtr
resulting number of namespace declarations
the element name parsed

briefdescription.para: Parse an end tag.

detaileddescription.para:
Always consumes '</'. [42] ETag ::= '</' Name S? '>' With namespace [NS 9] ETag ::= '</' QName S? '>'
ctxt
an XML parser context
tag
the corresponding start tag

briefdescription.para: Parse escaped pure raw content.

detaileddescription.para: Always consumes '<!['.

para.verbatim:
[18] CDSect ::= CDStart CData CDEnd
[19] CDStart ::= '<![CDATA['
[20] Data ::= (Char* - (Char* ']]>' Char*))
[21] CDEnd ::= ']]>'

briefdescription.para: Parse a content sequence.

detaileddescription.para: Stops at EOF or '</'. Leaves checking of unexpected EOF to the caller.

briefdescription.para: Parse XML element content.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you're only interested in custom SAX callbacks. If you want a node list, use xmlCtxtParseContent.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML element.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [39] element ::= EmptyElemTag | STag content ETag [ WFC: Element Type Match ] The Name in an element's end-tag must match the element type in the start-tag.

briefdescription.para: Parse the XML version value.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [26] VersionNum ::= '1.' [0-9]+ In practice allow [0-9].[0-9]+ at that level

simplesect.para: the string giving the XML version number, or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse the XML version.

para.verbatim:
[24] VersionInfo ::= S 'version' Eq (' VersionNum ' | " VersionNum ")
[25] Eq ::= S? '=' S?

simplesect.para: the version string, e.g. "1.0"

briefdescription.para: Parse the XML encoding name.

simplesect.para: the encoding name value or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse the XML encoding declaration.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [80] EncodingDecl ::= S 'encoding' Eq ('"' EncName '"' | "'" EncName "'") this setups the conversion filters.

simplesect.para: the encoding value or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse the XML standalone declaration.

detaileddescription.para:
Deprecated
Internal function, don't use. [32] SDDecl ::= S 'standalone' Eq (("'" ('yes' | 'no') "'") | ('"' ('yes' | 'no')'"')) [ VC: Standalone Document Declaration ] TODO The standalone document declaration must have the value "no" if any external markup declarations contain declarations of:
attributes with default values, if elements to which these attributes apply appear in the document without specifications of values for these attributes, or
entities (other than amp, lt, gt, apos, quot), if references to those entities appear in the document, or
attributes with values subject to normalization, where the attribute appears in the document with a value which will change as a result of normalization, or
element types with element content, if white space occurs directly within any instance of those types.

simplesect.para: 1 if standalone="yes" 0 if standalone="no" -2 if standalone attribute is missing or invalid (A standalone value of -2 means that the XML declaration was found, but no value was specified for the standalone attribute).

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML declaration header.

simplesect.para: the version from the XML declaration.
the value from the standalone document declaration.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML Misc* optional field.
Parse an XML document and invoke the SAX handlers.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you're only interested in custom SAX callbacks. If you want a document tree, use xmlCtxtParseDocument.

simplesect.para: 0, -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Parse a general parsed entity An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

simplesect.para: 0, -1 in case of error. the parser context is augmented as a result of the parsing.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the input buffer contains a character.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, size_t startDelta, const char *str, size_t strLen)
memberdef.name: xmlParseLookupString

briefdescription.para: Check whether the input buffer contains a string.

parameterdescription.para: delta to apply at the start

briefdescription.para: Check whether the input buffer contains terminated char data.
Check whether there's enough data in the input buffer to finish parsing a start tag.

detaileddescription.para: This has to take quotes into account.

briefdescription.para: Check whether there's enough data in the input buffer to finish parsing the internal subset.
Try to progress on parsing.

simplesect.para: zero if no parsing was possible

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *chunk, int size, int terminate)
memberdef.name: xmlParseChunk

briefdescription.para: Parse a chunk of memory in push parser mode.

detaileddescription.para: Assumes that the parser context was initialized with xmlCreatePushParserCtxt.

The last chunk, which will often be empty, must be marked with the terminate flag. With the default SAX callbacks, the resulting document will be available in ctxt->myDoc. This pointer will not be freed when calling xmlFreeParserCtxt and must be freed by the caller. If the document isn't well-formed, it will still be returned in ctxt->myDoc.
As an exception, xmlCtxtResetPush will free the document in ctxt->myDoc. So ctxt->myDoc should be set to NULL after extracting the document.
Since 2.14.0, xmlCtxtGetDocument can be used to retrieve the result document.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code (0 on success).

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, const char *chunk, int size, const char *filename)
memberdef.name: xmlCreatePushParserCtxt

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for using the XML parser in push mode.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlParseChunk.
Passing an initial chunk is useless and deprecated.
The push parser doesn't support recovery mode or the XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS option.
filename is used as base URI to fetch external entities and for error reports.

parameterdescription.para: size of initial chunk in bytes

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Blocks further parser processing.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, xmlCharEncoding enc)
memberdef.name: xmlCreateIOParserCtxt

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for using the XML parser with an existing I/O stream.

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse a DTD.

detaileddescription.para: Option XML_PARSE_DTDLOAD should be enabled in the parser context to make external entities work.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error. input will be freed by the function in any case.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse a DTD.

parameterdescription.para: the SAX handler block or NULL

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error. input will be freed by the function in any case.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse an external subset.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse an external subset.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserInputPtr input, int hasTextDecl, int buildTree)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtParseContentInternal

briefdescription.para: Parse an external general entity within an existing parsing context An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

parameterdescription.para: the URL for the entity to load
the System ID for the entity to load
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the entity is well formed, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *URL, const xmlChar *ID, xmlNode **list)
memberdef.name: xmlParseExternalEntity

briefdescription.para: Parse an external general entity An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the URL for the entity to load
the System ID for the entity to load
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the entity is well formed, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *string, xmlNode **lst)
memberdef.name: xmlParseBalancedChunkMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document called by the parser The allowed sequence for the Well Balanced Chunk is the one defined by the content production in the XML grammar:

detaileddescription.para: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to (must not be NULL)
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the input string in UTF8 or ISO-Latin (zero terminated)
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the chunk is well balanced, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of XML matching the 'content' production.

detaileddescription.para: Namespaces in scope of node and entities of node's document are recognized. When validating, the DTD of node's document is used.
Always consumes input even in error case.

simplesect.para: a node list or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlNode *node, const char *data, int datalen, int options, xmlNode **listOut)
memberdef.name: xmlParseInNodeContext

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document within the context (DTD, namespaces, etc ...) of the given node.

detaileddescription.para: The allowed sequence for the data is a Well Balanced Chunk defined by the content production in the XML grammar: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* This function assumes the encoding of node's document which is typically not what you want. A better alternative is xmlCtxtParseContent.

parameterdescription.para: the input string length in bytes
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: XML_ERR_OK if the chunk is well balanced, and the parser error code otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *string, xmlNode **listOut, int recover)
memberdef.name: xmlParseBalancedChunkMemoryRecover

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document.

detaileddescription.para: The allowed sequence for the Well Balanced Chunk is the one defined by the content production in the XML grammar: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* In case recover is set to 1, the nodelist will not be empty even if the parsed chunk is not well balanced, assuming the parsing succeeded to some extent.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to (must not be NULL)
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the input string in UTF8 or ISO-Latin (zero terminated)
the return value for the set of parsed nodes
return nodes even if the data is broken (use 0)

simplesect.para: 0 if the chunk is well balanced, or thehe parser error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML external entity out of context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.

Deprecated
Don't use. [78] extParsedEnt ::= TextDecl? content This correspond to a "Well Balanced" chunk
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML external entity out of context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: [78] extParsedEnt ::= TextDecl? content This correspond to a "Well Balanced" chunk
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for an external entity Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time.

parameterdescription.para: a possible base for the target URI

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for a file or URL content.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time and for file accesses

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for a file content.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time.

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.
User data (void *) is stored within the parser context in the context's _private member, so it is available nearly everywhere in libxml

parameterdescription.para: work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: In the case the document is not Well Formed, a attempt to build a tree is tried anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree if the file was wellformed, NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. In the case the document is not Well Formed, it attempts to build a tree anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Setup the parser context to parse a new buffer; Clears any prior contents from the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: The buffer parameter must not be NULL, but the filename parameter can be

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and call the given SAX handler routines.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: The user data returned on SAX callbacks

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or a error number otherwise

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for an XML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain a terminating null byte.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, const char *buffer, int size, int recovery, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlSAXParseMemoryWithData

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback.

detaileddescription.para: If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.
User data (void *) is stored within the parser context in the context's _private member, so it is available nearly everywhere in libxml

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, const char *buffer, int size, int recovery)
memberdef.name: xmlSAXParseMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback.

detaileddescription.para: If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read not Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: In the case the document is not Well Formed, an attempt to build a tree is tried anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory buffer and call the given SAX handler routines.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: The user data returned on SAX callbacks
the length of the XML document in bytes

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or a error number otherwise

briefdescription.para: Creates a parser context for an XML in-memory document.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Reset a parser context.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *chunk, int size, const char *filename, const char *encoding)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtResetPush

briefdescription.para: Reset a push parser context.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of chars
number of chars in the array
an optional file name or URI

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and 1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: Unset options are cleared.

2.13.0
With older versions, you can use xmlCtxtUseOptions.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get the current options of the parser context.

simplesect.para: the current options set in the parser context, or -1 if ctxt is NULL.

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: The following options are never cleared and can only be enabled:

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.

briefdescription.para: To protect against exponential entity expansion ("billion laughs"), the size of serialized output is (roughly) limited to the input size multiplied by this factor.

detaileddescription.para: The default value is 5.
When working with documents making heavy use of entity expansion, it can be necessary to increase the value. For security reasons, this should only be considered when processing trusted input.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document and return the resulting document tree.

detaileddescription.para: Takes ownership of the input object.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *cur, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an XML document from a zero-terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadDoc for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an XML file from the filesystem or a global, user-defined resource loader.

detaileddescription.para: If a "-" filename is passed, the function will read from stdin. This feature is potentially insecure and might be removed from later versions.
See xmlCtxtReadFile for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *buffer, int size, const char *url, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain a terminating null byte.
See xmlCtxtReadMemory for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadFd

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML from a file descriptor and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadFd for details.
NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadIO

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from I/O functions and context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadIO for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar *str, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *filename, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadFile

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file from the filesystem or a global, user-defined resource loader.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain a terminating null byte.
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadFd

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from a file descriptor and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadIO

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from I/O functions and source and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This reuses the existing ctxt parser context
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree
